verbGlossary.ORGANIZED BY: {source} breakdown structure follows {target} division hierarchy (e.g., schedule activities organized by MasterFormat divisions so cost and scope align to spec sections)
verbGlossary.CLASSIFIED UNDER: {source} element is categorized using {target} taxonomy (e.g., detail activity classified under a MasterFormat division to identify the trade or work scope it belongs to)
verbGlossary.MAPPED TO: {source}schedule element is cross-referenced to {target} element hierarchy (e.g., summary WBS activity mapped to a UniFormat assembly for element-based reporting)
verbGlossary.CODED BY: {source} is assigned a classification code from the target standard (e.g., activity code value drawn from {target} cost code list for cost tracking alignment)
verbGlossary.ASSIGNED TO: {source} is linked to a specific node in the target breakdown structure (e.g., detail activity assigned to a WBS code so progress and cost roll up correctly)
verbGlossary.DISTRIBUTED ACROSS: Cost-loaded schedule values are spread across {target} line items (e.g., cash flow projections distributed across budget codes for period-by-period forecasting)
verbGlossary.ALLOCATED AGAINST: {source}cost on {target} is charged to the target classification's account (e.g., labor and material cost loading allocated against the standard cost code for earned value measurement)
verbGlossary.MEASURED AGAINST: {source} specification compliance is evaluated relative to {target} acceptance criteria (e.g., schedule specification requirements measured against the project spec section for contractual conformance)
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED TO: {source} is compared against {target} published thresholds or industry norms (e.g., DCMA 14-point results benchmarked to AACE schedule quality standards)
verbGlossary.SCORED PER: {source} health composite metric is graded using {target} evaluation framework (e.g., schedule health score scored per AACE recommended practice thresholds)
verbGlossary.TRIGGERED BY: {source}is created because {target} code requires an inspection or approval at that construction stage (e.g., foundation milestone triggered by IBC required inspection hold point)
verbGlossary.GOVERNED BY: {source} or delay classification follows rules defined in {target} or contract standard (e.g., schedule constraint dates governed by IBC occupancy permit prerequisites, delay types governed by AIA contract provisions)
verbGlossary.SEQUENCED FOR: {source}are ordered to satisfy {target} prerequisite inspections or certification requirements (e.g., fire protection activities sequenced for NFPA testing milestones, envelope activities sequenced for energy code compliance testing)
verbGlossary.TAGGED TO: {source} is linked to {target} for cross-domain traceability (e.g., detail activity tagged to an OmniClass work result for BIM coordination and reporting)
verbGlossary.LINKED TO: {source}schedule element is associated with {target} model objects (e.g., 4D simulation activities linked to IFC entity classes for clash-free sequencing)
verbGlossary.MODELED AGAINST: {source}schedule maps construction sequences to {target} classification (e.g., 4D schedule phases modeled against UniFormat assemblies for visual progress tracking)
verbGlossary.SUBMITTED PER: {source}document is prepared and submitted following {target} standard's required procedures and notice periods (e.g., time extension request submitted per AIA A201 Article 15 claim provisions)
verbGlossary.SEGMENTED BY: {source} or analysis is broken down by {target} for comparative insight (e.g., time impact analysis segmented by delivery method to compare delay exposure across project types)
verbGlossary.PHASED FOR: {source}schedule phases are structured to align with the target certification's prerequisite sequencing and credit submittal milestones (e.g., commissioning activities phased for LEED fundamental commissioning prerequisite deadlines)
